reason: MVP PRD with all 8 brainstorm decisions: subcommand surface, file-or-stdin I/O, TTY progress, unset budget default, std exit-code error protocol, TS+esbuild scaffold, manual sync from ref. Source of truth for implementation.
Charter ADR-lite — four project-level principles (runtime-transparent, thin-shell upstream sync, no-rerendering, self-contained output) that the MVP MUST not violate.
Spike findings (6 architecture decisions). Implementation must align with: --output-format=text suffices, extractHtml ports as-is, --bare default, zero-deps Node viable, TTY progress needed.
100-line working end-to-end reference implementation. PR2 (render+claude) must produce equivalent behavior in TypeScript.
Project charter: positioning, pain points, MVP capability list, roadmap. Implementation scope must match capability list.
ref/ read-only semantics + Apache-2.0 attribution rule. Any code ported from ref/ must include upstream-path + commit comment header.
SHARED_DESIGN_DIRECTIVES + assemblePrompt — must be ported verbatim into src/templates/shared.ts (the prompt assembly contract).
parseFrontmatter / listSkills / loadSkill — port verbatim into src/templates/loader.ts. SKILLS_DIR const must be rewired to src/templates/skills (post-sync location).
extractHtml + previewHtml — spike confirmed zero-modification port. Place at src/extract-html.ts.
buildArgv reference — only the `case 'claude'` branch is needed for MVP. Spike showed simpler `--output-format=text + --bare` already works; do not blindly copy stream-json wiring.
Apache-2.0 — required attribution for any code ported from ref/. Ported files must include source path + commit hash in header.
